focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 18

The hum was quieter on this side of the valley.

Not gone — the hum was never gone, not since Fenhold, not since the first morning he could remember hearing it — but stretched thin, like water across too much stone. Three days past the Woundmouth and Maren's body still hadn't forgiven him. His knees ground when he bent them. The muscles along his ribs ached in a deep, wrung-out way, as if someone had reached inside his trunk and twisted everything a quarter turn to the left. But his hands were steady. That was new.

The road — what passed for a road this deep, where dungeon territory gave way to farmland and the path widened from packed dirt to actual ruts — had scorch marks in the earth. Old ones, layered dark over darker, from heroes who'd passed through seasons ago. Nobody cleaned them. Nobody ever cleaned them. Early autumn had crept in while he wasn't paying attention, and the light hit those burn marks differently now, cooler, less shimmer and more of a pale, flat quality that made the road look tired.

*The contamination index has dropped significantly since your... intervention,* Aldric said. He paused on "intervention," and Maren braced out of habit for the insult that never came. *The residual levels in this stretch are within normal seasonal parameters.*

"So it worked."

*I believe I just said that.*

"You said 'contamination index' and 'seasonal parameters.' I said 'it worked.'"

A pause. Then, quieter: *Yes. It worked.*

Maren looked down the road. Easier than answering that.

The early-autumn air sat in the back of his throat with a crispness that wasn't unpleasant — just present, like a taste he couldn't quite place. The fields on either side of the road had been harvested recently. Cut stubble scratched at his ankles where the road narrowe...
open_hooks_to_carry: The Woundmouth: a dungeon mouth where quest-light bleeds rather than pools, contamination so old it has become structural
Maren seeing the System interface for the first and only time — light, numbers, framework — and behind it, the machinery
The System as desperate containment: not malevolent, not benevolent, but holding something wilder and older in check
Maren as both crack and sealant — the only one who can touch both sides because the System cannot write on him
The consumption nearly killing him; Aldric holding him together through stubbornness, not heroism
Maren choosing to maintain the wall rather than break it — a practical decision, not a heroic one

payoffs_to_land.description: The full revelation of the System's nature, seeded through Aldric's observations and the hero-town architecture: it is a containment structure, and what it contains is the unclassifiable world that preceded it

prev_tail_excerpt:
...aining addressed the foundational techniques. What you have lost is...* He stopped. *The habit of sitting where you can see an entrance. The impulse to touch the knife before you have even located the threat. Her specific observations about—*

He stopped again. Maren waited. The night bird didn't call.

*She was wrong frequently.* Aldric said it with the cadence of a man reaching for a familiar position and finding that it no longer fit his hand. *About matters of moral consequence. Her classification training prioritized expedience over—*

He stopped a third time.

*She was not wrong about this.*

Maren put his chin on his knees. The stone wall pressed cold against his back. His hands had stopped shaking at some point during the ritual — the first time in two days — and the stillness of his own fingers against his shins was strange, like touching the place where an ache had lived and finding it gone. The headache behind his left eye was gone too. The wrong-frequency vibration in his chest had narrowed into something simpler, steadier — still the unclassified entity, still the thing the System couldn't name, but no longer scraping itself raw inside him.

He missed her already.

"She didn't say goodbye," he said. "Not exactly. She said the contract was released and she said something about her sister and then she was just — not here."

*She said things once.* Aldric. Quiet. *You knew this about her.*

"Yeah."

*Then you know that was the goodbye.*

Maren stayed in the sheep pen until the cold worked through his shirt. Aldric didn't speak again. In the morning he would get up and keep walking east toward the Woundmouth with the bowl in his pack, one voice in his skull, and the simpler thing in his chest. He would be less than he had been, but stable enough to keep moving.

setups_to_plant.description: The hero-town infrastructure in Chapter 9 and Aldric's architectural description in Chapter 12 resolve here as the full picture of the System's purpose

state_deltas_required: The Woundmouth — the thinnest point in the System's architecture — where Maren must consume the System's wound itself to understand what he is
The mystery resolves: the System is desperate containment, Maren is both the crack and the only one who can seal it; he chooses maintenance over destruction
